programmes.show_description: 1960. Horror. Directed by Ernest Morris. Stars Laurence Payne, Adrienne Corri & Dermot Walsh. A man is tortured by the sound of his friend's beating heart after killing him in a jealous rage.

programmes.show_name: The Man with the Golden Arm
programmes.show_description: 1955. Drama. Directed by Otto Preminger. Stars Frank Sinatra, Kim Novak & Eleanor Parker. The story of a recovering drug addict who must battle to stay clean after being released from prison.
1965. A Neat Little Package. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. A newsdealer survives a car wreck with amnesia and a mysterious $175,000.
1935. Western. Director: Harry S. Webb. Stars Jack Perrin, Fred Humes, Philo McCullough. Cactus Jack is blamed for murder after his partner is found with a knife in his back.
Made in 1934 screen songs animated short. Produced by Fleischer Studios. Director Dave Fleischer. The song featured in this cartoon was 'I'm Popeye The Sailor Man written by Sammy Lerner.
Trapped. 1974. Stars John Sanderson, Roberta Tovey & Mark Donaldson. Peterkin remains trapped inside Lady Ann's cottage. Meanwhile, Lord William barges in on Lady Ann's wedding.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Dean and Thabo are in serious trouble until the young princess of the hidden city helps them.
The Black Castle. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Torwald holds Lord Trebizond to ransom in the Black Castle. Lorraine, rides to Camelot to seek help.
Ice Spy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze is back in town and he plans to build an icy weapon that will have the world at his control.
Hidden Treasure. 1954. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger & Dale Evans. After overhearing Dale tell of where great treasure can be found, the outlaws will stop at nothing to get their hands on it.
Winged Assassin. 1967. Stars Ed Bishop, Jeremy Wilkin & Francis Matthews. Spectrum Organisation attempts to stop the Mysterons from assassinating the Director General of the United Asian Republic.
Boy Slaves. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Can Tell trust the word of his enemy? He must, if he's to obtain the release of young boys whom Gessler is holding.
The Articles of War. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Eric Pohlmann & Brian Rawlinson. The island is running out of food and a Spanish naval ship threatens to destroy the next shipment.
Tables Turned. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. Messengers are guarding the jewels, but loose talking allows Derwent to her about it; so, he plans to steal them!
1965. Sci-fi. Directed by Koreyoshi Akasaka, Teruo Ishii & Akira Mitsuwa. Stars Ken Utsui & Sachihiro Ohsawa. Starman is sent to Earth after learning of a devastating plot to destroy the planet.
1947. Crime Drama. Directed by John Paddy Carstairs. Stars Richard Attenborough, Barry K. Barnes, Sheila Sim & Garry Marsh. A Cab driver gets mistaken with a gang. He sets out to expose the group.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Gold in the Mud
programmes.show_description: 1969. An interesting look at the world of scampi which has increased in popularity.
Escape. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Jack Lambert & Harry Towmb. Following a tip-off, Bill Killian is caught opening a safe. His brother helps him escape but the police are hot on their trail.
The Twisted Road. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Tom Farley. Cobb must deliver a prisoner safely to Brisbane in order for him to face charges after being accused of murder.
1958. War. Guy Green. Stars Richard Attenborough, Michael Craig & John Gregson. The Long Range Desert Group go on an expedition through North Africa to destroy a huge Nazi fuel dump.
A late 50s local authority travelogue showcasing the wonders of the great Scottish city of Aberdeen.
Seven Little Crosses. 1962. Stars Rupert Davies, Alfred Burke & James Maxwell. The young son of a murder suspect leads the police on a clever trail. Is he purposely hindering or helping the case?

programmes.show_name: Sherlock Holmes: Hound of the Baskervilles
programmes.show_description: 1988. Drama. Director: Brian Mills. Stars Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke & Raymond Adamson. Can Sherlock find the truth when the heir to the Baskerville estate is threatened?
1953. Thriller. Director: Hugo Fregonese. Stars Jack Palance, Constance Smith & Byron Palmer. A landlady suspects her new tenant is Jack the Ripper when a self-described pathologist moves in.
Short documentary film detailing Hollywood's fascination with the heavenly, the devilish and the supernatural, including the Topper film series and It's A Wonderful Life
1994. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. Pamela Anderson and Eva are at the Cannes Film Festival and take a look around Monaco with model Karen Mulder.
Drama. Director: John Hillcoat. Stars John Hurt, Guy Pearce & Ray Winstone. A lawman gives an outlaw nine days to kill his older brother, or they'll execute his younger brother.
1977. Director: Herb Wallerstein. Stars Bo Svenson, Yvette Mimieux & Robert Logan. A monstrous creature stalks and kills visitors of Colorado ski resort. The fight for survival begins.
Caledon. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. On a mission of friendship to an ally and carrying a priceless gift from his King, Sir Lancelot is attacked and left to die.
Titan Goes Pop. 1964. Stars Don Mason, Lois Maxwell & David Graham. When pop star Duke Dexter pays a visit to Marineville, Surface Agent X20 decides to abduct the singer and take him to Titan.
1965. The Owl and The Eye. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Honey must steal a jade carving from a museum.
1978. Sci-fi Comedy. Director: Norman Tokar. Stars Ken Berry, Sandy Duncan & Harry Morgan. A physicist and his girlfriend try to help an extraterrestrial space cat fix his ship and return home.
1941. Action. Directors: John English & William Witney. Stars Ralph Byrd, Michael Owen & Jan Wiley. Dick Tracy goes up against a villain known as The Ghost, who can turn himself invisible.
1945. Crime. Director: Montgomery Tully. Stars William Hartnell, Jimmy Hanley & Chili Bouchier. After a long imprisonment, a man wrongfully framed for murder hunts for the truth and the killer.
The Race for Queen. 1964. Stars Donna Douglas, Robert Cummings & Susan Hart. Elly May decides to enter a beauty competition to become 'The Queen of Beverly Hills', but Granny also wants to enter.
1948. Drama. Director: Roy Boulting. Stars Richard Attenborough, Bernard Miles & Sheila Sim. Set after WW2. Jack Read, a cockney boy, becomes a guinea pig in an educational experiment.
Batman's Waterloo. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. With Batman still in his watery trap, Robin is left in the hands of King Tut. Will Batman be able to save them?
The Room. 1953. Stars Dick Powell & Raymond Burr. A doctor opens an office in a small Cuban village to find out doctors are not allowed according to the decree of a 200 year old unseen tyrant.
1960. A glimpse at some of the people in Britain who have something to protest about, including a look at the Aldermaston marchers and Speaker's Corner.
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Richard Baker, Honor Blackman, Derek Griffiths, Gloria Hunniford, Jenny Lee-Wright & Alfred Mark.
The Neptune Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo seeks to find a group of scientists who are trying to start World War 3.
Mike and Noel travel back to 1960 for a rare reel; Hughie Green's Happy Travellers Club. Enjoy a beautiful home movie of Venice from 1963 and we jet across the pond to see Paul Anka!
Act of Riot. 1988. Stars Robert Powell, Christopher Scoular & Gavin Richards. Hannay returns to Scotland to visit the village where he was born, but his arrival quickly brings him into danger.
Driven Apart. 1981. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Robert is starting to regret teaching Angie how to drive when she hits another car. As usual Ida saves the day.
The Bargain. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Joy Adamson & Bryan Forbes. Clifton enlists the help of his attractive wife to secure information from a business associate and is surprised by the offer.
The Case of the Dumb Witness. 1964. Stars John Barrie & Warren Mitchell. Sergeant Cork is called to help with a spate of burglaries but Leavis is unconvinced by his methods.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Joe Baker, Alan Clive and Joan Savage play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1974. Comedy. Directed by Val Guest. Stars Anthony Booth, Robin Askwith & Dandy Nichols. The adventures of an optimistic and inept window cleaner who finds other ways to satisfy his customers.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
